volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 2.1074 - Section 1260)

sections.label: Section 2.1074
sections.translation: I blush for that god of song ,

sections.label: Section 2.1075
sections.original: μνον θεόν , εἰ παρὰ καλλιχόροισι παγαῖς
sections.translation: if this stranger is to witness the torch-dance , that heralds in the twentieth dawn , around Callichorus ’ fair springs , a sleepless votary in midnight revels , what time the star-lit firmament of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 2.1080
sections.translation: the moon , and Nereus ’ fifty daughters , that trip it lightly o ’ er the sea and the eternal rivers ’ tides , join the dance in honour

sections.label: Section 2.1085
sections.translation: of the maiden with the crown of gold and her majestic mother ; where this vagabond , by Phoebus favoured , thinks to reign , entering into other men ’ s hard toil .

sections.label: Section 2.1090
sections.translation: Look to it , all ye bards , who , in malicious strains , expose our amours and unholy bonds of lawless love ; see how far our

sections.label: Section 2.1095
sections.translation: virtue surpasses man ’ s disloyalty . Change the burden of your song and keep your spiteful verse to brand man ’ s faithlessness . For this scion of the stock of Zeus

sections.label: Section 2.1100
sections.translation: shows himself a heedless wight , denying to the mistress of his halls the lot of mutual offspring , and , paying all his court to some strange love ,

sections.label: Section 2.1105
sections.translation: hath gotten him a bastard son .

sections.label: Section 1106
sections.translation: Ladies of another land , where may I find your mistress , daughter of Erechtheus ? For I have searched each nook and corner of this town , and cannot find her .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν , ὦ ξύνδουλε ; τίς προθυμία
sections.translation: What news , my fellow-thrall ? why that hurried gait ? what tidings bringest thou ?

sections.label: Section 1111
sections.translation: I am pursued ; the rulers of this land are seeking her to stone her to death .

sections.label: Section 1113
sections.original: οἴμοι , τί λέξεις ; οὔτι που λελήμμεθα
sections.translation: Alas , what is thy tale ? say not we are detected in our secret plot for murdering the boy ?

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: ἔγνως ; — μεθέξεις οὐκ ἐν ὑστάτοις κακοῦ .
sections.translation: Thou hast guessed aright ; nor wilt thou be the last to share the trouble .

sections.label: Section 1116
sections.original: ὤφθη δὲ πῶς τὰ κρυπτὰ μηχανήματα ;
sections.translation: How was the hidden scheme laid bare ?

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: τὸ μὴ δίκαιον τῆς δίκης ἡσσώμενον
sections.translation: The god found means to master wrong with right , unwilling to see his shrine polluted .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: πῶς ; ἀντιάζω σʼ ἱκέτις ἐξειπεῖν τάδε .
sections.translation: How so ? I do conjure thee , tell us all .

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: πεπυσμέναι γάρ , εἰ θανεῖν ὅμως χρεών ,
sections.translation: For if to die or yet to live be ours , ’ twere sweeter so , when we know all .

sections.label: Section 1122
sections.translation: Soon as Xuthus , husband of Creusa , had left the god ’ s prophetic shrine , taking with him his new-found son , to hold the feast and sacrifice that he designed to offer to the gods ,

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: Ξοῦθος μὲν ᾤχετʼ ἔνθα πῦρ πηδᾷ θεοῦ
sections.translation: himself departed to the place where leaps the Bacchic flame , with blood of sacrifice to dew the double peaks of Dionysus for the son now offered to his gaze , and thus he spake , My son , abide thou here , and raise a spacious tent by craftsmen ’ s toiling skill ;

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: θύσας δὲ Γενέταις Θεοῖσιν ἢν μακρὸν χρόνον
sections.translation: and if I remain long time away after I have sacrificed to the gods of thy birth , let the feast be spread for all friends present .

sections.label: Section 1132
sections.original: λαβὼν δὲ μόσχους ᾤχεθʼ · ὁ δὲ νεανίας
sections.translation: Therewith he took the heifers and went his way . Meantime his stripling son in solemn form set up with upright stays the tent , inclosed but not with walls ,

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: καλῶς φυλάξας , οὔτε πρὸς μέσας φλογὸς
sections.translation: taking good heed to guard it ’ gainst the blazing midday sun , nor less against his westering beams , the limit of his course ; an oblong space of five score feet he meted out so that it contained ten thousand feet within that measure ’ s square , as science phrases it ,

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: ὡς πάντα Δελφῶν λαὸν ἐς θοίνην καλῶν .
sections.translation: intending to invite all Delphi to the feast . Then from the temple-treasury tapestry he took and therewith made a shelter , wondrous sight to see . First o ’ er the roof-tree he threw a canopy of robes , an offering Heracles , the son of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.translation: had brought unto the god from his Amazonian spoils . On them was broidered many a pictured scene , to wit , Heaven marshalling his host of stars upon the vaulted sky ; there was the sun-god urging on his steeds toward his fiery goal , the bright star of evening at his heels .

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.translation: Night too in sable robes went hurrying by , drawn by a single pair , and the stars did bear her company . Across the zenith a Pleiad sailed , and Orion too with falchion dight was there ; above was the bear making his tail to turn upon the golden pole .

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.translation: Up shot the moon ’ s full face , that parts the months in twain ; there too the Hyades showed their unerring light to mariners ; arid Dawn , that brings the morning back , was chasing the stars before her . Next on the sides he hung yet other tapestry ;

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.translation: barbarian ships bearing down on the fleet of Hellas ; and monsters half-man , half-beast ; the capture of the Thracian steeds ; the hunting of savage stags and lions fierce ; while at the entry Cecrops close to his daughters was wreathing his coils , an offering of some Athenian

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: ἀνάθημα · χρυσέους τʼ ἐν μέσῳ συσσιτίῳ
sections.translation: votary ; and in the midst of the banquet-hall he set goblets of gold , while a herald hasted and invited to the feast all citizens who would come . Then , when the tent was full , they decked themselves with garlands and took their fill

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: ψυχὴν ἐπλήρουν . ὡς δʼ ἀνεῖσαν ἡδονήν ,
sections.translation: of the rich viands . Anon after they had put from them the pleasure of eating came an old man and stood in the midst , where his officious zeal provoked loud laughter among the guests ; for he would draw from the drinking-pitchers water to wash the hands withal and

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.translation: was wasting as incense the liquid myrrh , and in his charge he took the golden beakers , setting himself unasked to this office .

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἐς αὐλοὺς ἧκον ἐς κρατῆρά τε
sections.translation: Now when they were come to the time for the flute-players and the general libation , cried out that aged servitor , Hence with these tiny cups ! bring larger goblets ,

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: ὡς θᾶσσον ἔλθωσʼ οἵδʼ ἐς ἡδονὰς φρενῶν .
sections.translation: that our guests may find a quicker route to joyousness . Thereon came servants bending ’ neath the weight of goblets chased with silver and golden chalices ; and that old man , as if to do his youthful lord a special service , chose out and offered to him a brimming bumper , when he had cast into the wine

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.translation: that potent philtre which , men say , his mistress gave to him to end the young man ’ s days on earth ; and no man knew of this ; but just as he so lately found held in his hand the drink-offering , the others following suit , some servant there uttered a word of evil import ;

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: ὁ δʼ , ὡς ἐν ἱερῷ μάντεσίν τʼ ἐσθλοῖς τραφείς ,
sections.translation: whereat the stripling , as one who had been reared within the shrine amid reputed seers , deemed this an omen and bade them fill a fresh goblet , but that first drink-offering to the god he poured upon the ground and bade all others do the like . And silence stole upon them ;

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.translation: while we with water and Phoenician wine were filling high the sacred bowls . While thus we were busied , comes a flight of doves and settles in the tent , for these dwell fearlessly in the courts of Loxias . Soon as the guests had poured away the luscious juice , those thirsty birds did dip their beaks therein ,

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: καθῆκαν , εἷλκον δʼ εὐπτέρους ἐς αὐχένας .
sections.translation: drawing it up into their feathered throats .

sections.label: Section 1201
sections.original: καὶ ταῖς μὲν ἄλλαις ἄνοσος ἦν λοιβὴ θεοῦ ·
sections.translation: Now all the rest received no hurt from the god ’ s libation , but one that settled on the spot where the son newfound had poured his wine , no sooner had tasted thereof , than convulsions seized her feathered form and she went triad , and screaming aloud uttered

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.translation: strange unwonted cries ; and all the feasters gathered there marvelled to see the bird ’ s cruel agony , for she lay writhing in the toils of death , and her red claws relaxed their hold .

sections.label: Section 1207
sections.translation: Forthwith the son , vouchsafed by oracles , bared his arm by casting off his cloak and stretched it out across the board

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: βοᾷ δέ · Τίς μʼ ἔμελλεν ἀνθρώπων κτενεῖν ;
sections.translation: crying Who was it strove to slay me ? Proclaim it , old sirrah , for thine was the officious zeal and thine the hand from which I took the cup . With that he caught the grey-beard by the arm and set to searching him that he might take the old man red-handed in the act .

sections.label: Section 1215
sections.original: ὤφθη δὲ καὶ κατεῖπʼ ἀναγκασθεὶς μόγις
sections.translation: So was he detected , and under strong constraint declared Creusa ’ s daring deed and all the trick of the poisoned draught . Forth rushed the young man , whom the oracle of Loxias to his sire assigned , taking with him the banqueters , and standing mid the Delphic nobles made harangue ,

sections.label: Section 1220
sections.original: Ὦ Γαῖα σεμνή , τῆς Ἐρεχθέως ὕπο ,
sections.translation: O ! hallowed soil , a stranger woman , daughter of Erechtheus , seeks to poison me . And the lords of Delphi decreed by general vote that my mistress should be hurled from the rock to die , because she strove to slay the priest and compass his death in the temple .

sections.label: Section 1225
sections.original: φόνον τιθεῖσαν . πᾶσα δὲ ζητεῖ πόλις
sections.translation: So now is the whole city seeking her , who hath to her sorrow sped a hapless journey ; for , coming to crave the boon of offspring from Phoebus , she hath lost her life and children too .

sections.label: Section 1229
sections.translation: Ah me ! I see no way at all to turn death ’ s

sections.label: Section 1230
sections.translation: hand aside ; all , all , ere this , is brought to light owing to that fatal draught of the wine-god ’ s juice mixed for death with drops of viper ’ s gore , quick to slay ;

sections.label: Section 1235
sections.translation: detected is our offering to the dead ; for me my life must end in woe , while death by stoning waits my mistress . How can I escape ? Shall I take wings and fly away , or creep beneath the darksome caverns of the earth ,

sections.label: Section 1240
sections.translation: striving to shun the doom of death by stoning ? or shall I mount the car drawn by swiftest steeds , or embark upon a ship ?

sections.label: Section 1244
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι λαθεῖν , ὅτε μὴ χρῄζων
sections.translation: No man may hide his guilt , save when some

sections.label: Section 1245
sections.translation: god of his own will steals him away . Ah ! my poor mistress ! what suffering now awaits thy soul ? Must then our wish to work another harm end in our own discomfiture , as justice doth decree ?

sections.label: Section 1250
sections.translation: My trusty maids , the men of death are on my track ; the vote of Delphi goes against me ; they give me up to die .

sections.label: Section 1252
sections.original: ἴσμεν , ὦ τάλαινα , τὰς σὰς συμφοράς , ἵνʼ εἶ τύχης .
sections.translation: Unhappy one ! we know thy sad mischance , how thou art placed .

sections.label: Section 1253
sections.original: ποῖ φύγω δῆτʼ ; ἐκ γὰρ οἴκων προύλαβον μόγις πόδα
sections.translation: Oh ! whither can I fly ? for scarce had I the start of my pursuers from the house in my race for life ; ’ tis by stealth alone that I have thus far escaped my foes .

sections.label: Section 1255
sections.original: ποῖ δʼ ἂν ἄλλοσʼ ἢ ʼπὶ βωμόν ;
sections.translation: Where shouldst thou fly except to the altar ?

sections.label: Section 1255b
sections.translation: What good is that to me ?

sections.label: Section 1256
sections.translation: To slay a suppliant is forbidden .

sections.label: Section 1256b
sections.translation: Aye , but the law has given me over to death .

sections.label: Section 1257
sections.translation: Only if thou fall into their hands .

sections.label: Section 1257b
sections.translation: Look ! here they come , cruel champions of vengeance , eagerly brandishing their swords .

sections.label: Section 1258b
sections.translation: Sit thee down upon the altar of burnt-offering ! for if thou art slain there , thou wilt fix

sections.label: Section 1260
sections.original: προστρόπαιον αἷμα θήσεις · οἰστέον δὲ τὴν τύχην .
sections.translation: upon thy murderers the stain of bloodguiltiness ; but we must bear our fortune .
